title: The Making of an Action Star in 2019, Plus: Reviving Action-Comedy and 'Stuber' With Director Michael Dowse | Interview
format: Fennessey and Shoemaker trace the wrestler-to-movie-star pipeline that runs through Dave Bautista, then Stuber director Michael Dowse breaks down FUBAR, Goon, and what it takes to fire on both cylinders in an action comedy.

segments.heading: The Making of an Action Star in 2019
segments.note: Sean Fennessey and David Shoemaker on wrestlers who became movie stars

films.title: Stuber
films.note: Kumail Nanjiani and Dave Bautista's new action comedy is the hook for the whole conversation about wrestlers making the jump to Hollywood.

films.title: Road House
films.note: Shoemaker's go-to for Terry Funk: funny in it without really trying to be.

films.title: The Scorpion King 3: Battle for Redemption
films.note: Bautista's early role as Argamael, made while literally following The Rock's old franchise.

films.title: The Man with the Iron Fists
films.note: Bautista played Brass Body in RZA's kung-fu movie.

films.title: Riddick
films.note: Bautista played Diaz — one more entry in a run of parts before Guardians changed his career.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Drax the Destroyer is the role that changed everything for Bautista, even if the odd part after it didn't show it yet.

films.title: L.A. Slasher
films.note: Bautista played 'drug dealer number one' — one of the stranger stops on his way up.

films.title: The Warrior's Gate
films.note: Bautista played Aaron the Cruel.

films.title: Bushwick
films.note: A not-bad action movie where Bautista played Stoop.

films.title: Blade Runner 2049
films.note: Bautista's Sapper Morton is what Dowse says actually sold him on casting Bautista in Stuber, not Drax.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Mentioned as more evidence Bautista is being cast for presence, not size alone.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Cited twice: once for how recessed and serious The Rock plays it, once as Roman Reigns's own audition for movie stardom.

films.title: Red Notice
films.note: Discussed here as the then-unmade Netflix pickup from Universal, reportedly derailed at Universal by The Rock's $21 million salary ask plus 30% of profits.

films.title: Stay Hungry
films.note: Bob Rafelson's film is cited as Schwarzenegger's first big role, shot around the same time as Pumping Iron.

films.title: They Live
films.note: Shoemaker's example of Roddy Piper's stoic, un-Piper-like screen persona versus his loose-cannon wrestling character.

films.title: Stuber
films.note: Dowse's new Kumail Nanjiani/Dave Bautista action comedy, his biggest budget to date and his first Disney release since the Fox acquisition.

films.title: True Romance
films.note: The first film Dowse remembers checking the director's name for afterward — Tony Scott directing a Tarantino script.

films.title: Man Bites Dog
films.note: The Belgian fake-documentary that showed Dowse a film could be made with three or four people and no money — the seed for FUBAR.

films.title: FUBAR
films.note: Dowse's own no-budget, largely improvised debut about headbangers, made at 28 and edited on stolen studio time.

films.title: It's All Gone Pete Tong
films.note: The Ibiza film that was Dowse's jump from a half-million-dollar debut to a real budget, landed off the strength of FUBAR.

films.title: Goon
films.note: Dowse's violent hockey comedy, made partly as a corrective to how campy hockey movies had gotten.

films.title: Slap Shot
films.note: One of the hockey films Dowse looked back at while shaping Goon's tone.

films.title: North Dallas Forty
films.note: The other reference point for Goon's grounded, unglamorous sports-movie vibe.

films.title: The Longest Yard
films.note: Cited alongside Slap Shot as the kind of authentic, funny sports movie Dowse wanted to recapture.

films.title: Miracle
films.note: Dowse's one exception to his complaint that most hockey movies are too campy.

films.title: 48 Hrs.
films.note: Top of Dowse's Action Comedy Hall of Fame — a film that wasn't even conceived as a comedy until Eddie Murphy was cast.

films.title: Midnight Run
films.note: Another Dowse Action Comedy Hall of Famer.

films.title: Lethal Weapon
films.note: Another Dowse Action Comedy Hall of Famer.

films.title: Running Scared
films.note: Dowse's word: 'probably a pretty good one.'

films.title: Beverly Hills Cop
films.note: Like 48 Hrs., another Eddie Murphy vehicle that wasn't written as a comedy first — they'd considered Sylvester Stallone for the lead.

films.title: Game Night
films.note: Dowse calls it one of the best comedies of the last five years, made by Stuber producers Goldstein and Daley.

films.title: The Big Sick
films.note: What convinced Dowse Kumail Nanjiani could carry a movie, dramatically and comedically.

films.title: Coffee & Kareem
films.note: The action comedy Dowse shot for Netflix right after Stuber, starring Ed Helms and Taraji P. Henson.

films.title: The Wolf's Call
films.note: Dowse's answer to the last-great-thing-you've-seen closer: a French submarine thriller.

films.title: Tootsie
films.note: Rewatched recently with his kids — Dowse calls it 'the coolest Woody Allen movie he never made' and says nobody should ever remake it.

The Scorpion King 3: Battle for Redempti.title: The Scorpion King 3: Battle for Redemption

The Man with the Iron Fists.title: The Man with the Iron Fists
